Asparagus is a plant belonging to the Asparagaceae family, specifically a perennial flowering plant that is native to Europe and Asia, known for its edible shoots.

Asparagus has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the digestive system. The bioactive constituents of Asparagus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its potential health benefits.

Asparagus is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, as well as being used as a food ingredient in various cuisines around the world.
